title: Improvements in or relating to tractor-baler combinations
abstract: A moveable tractor-baler combination (10) includes (a) a self-powered tractor (11) having a plurality of ground-engaging members (13, 14, 16, 17) and a steering mechanism for steering at least one said ground-engaging member so as to cause changes in a direction of movement of the tractor (11); (b) a baler (12) that is towed behind the tractor (11) as the tractor (11) moves forwardly and is pivotably connected to the tractor (11); (c) one or more sensors (36, 38) for sensing one or more lines (herein swath lines) of crop material corresponding to a maximal quantity of crop material per unit length of a swath (24); and (d) a control apparatus (41). The control apparatus (41) operates in dependence on at least one output of the one or more sensors (36, 38) to operate the steering mechanism of the tractor (11) such that the baler (12) follows a said swath line in a manner optimising the ingestion of crop material into the baler (12) for baling. One or more said sensors (36, 38) is at least temporarily operable to sense a swath line that is laterally offset from the direction of forward movement of the tractor (11). As a result the alignment of the baler (12) may be optimised before baling of a swath (24) commences.
claims:
Claims 1 . A moveable tractor-baler combination (10) including (a) a self-powered tractor (1 1 ) having a plurality of ground-engaging members (13, 14, 16, 17) and a steering mechanism for steering at least one said ground-engaging member so as to cause changes in a direction of movement of the tractor (1 1 ); (b) a baler (12) that is towed behind the tractor (1 1 ) when the tractor (1 1 ) moves forwardly and is pivotably connected to the tractor (1 1 ); (c) one or more sensors (36, 38) for sensing one or more lines (herein swath lines) of crop material corresponding to a maximal quantity of crop material per unit length of a swath (24); and (d) a control apparatus (41 ) that operates in dependence on at least one output of the one or more sensors (36, 38) to operate the steering mechanism of the tractor such that the baler follows a said swath line in a manner optimising the ingestion of crop material into the baler (12) for baling, wherein one or more said sensors (36, 38) is at least temporarily operable to sense a swath line that is laterally offset from the direction of forward movement (44) of the tractor (1 1 ). 2. A tractor-baler combination (10) according to Claim 1 wherein one or more said sensor (36) is moveable between a first orientation in which it points forwardly of the tractor (1 1 ) and a second orientation in which it points sideways of the tractor (1 1 ). 3. A tractor-baler combination (10) according to Claim 2 including a pivotable mounting securing at least one said sensor (36) relative to the tractor, the pivotable mounting permitting one or more degrees of freedom of movement of the sensor (36). 4. A tractor-baler combination (10) according to Claim 2 or Claim 3 including one or more motors for effecting movement of at least one said sensor (36) between the first orientation and the second orientation. 5. A tractor-baler combination (10) according to Claim 1 wherein the one or more sensors (36, 38) include respective first (36) and second (38) sensors for sensing one or more swath lines, the first sensor (36) pointing forwardly of the tractor (1 1 ) and the second sensor (38) pointing sideways of the tractor (1 1 ) and the orientations of the first and second sensors (36, 38) being fixed with respect to the tractor (1 1 ). 6. A tractor-baler combination (10) according to Claim 5 wherein the first sensor (36) is moveable between a first orientation in which it points forwardly of the tractor (1 1 ) and a second orientation in which it points laterally with respect to the tractor (1 1 ). 7. A tractor-baler combination (10) according to any preceding claim wherein at least one said sensor (36, 38) is mounted on the tractor (1 1 ). 8. A tractor-baler combination (10) according to any preceding claim wherein one or more said sensor (36, 38) is operatively connected to the control apparatus (41 ) whereby on the said sensor (36, 38) sensing a swath (24) that is laterally offset from the direction of forward movement of the tractor (1 1 ) the control apparatus (41 ) operates the steering mechanism of the tractor (1 1 ) to cause steering of the tractor-baler combination (10) such that the baler (12) aligns for ingestion of crop material corresponding to the swath (24). 9. A tractor-baler combination (10) according to any preceding claim wherein at least one said sensor (36, 38) is an optical device that generates an output derived from a two- dimensional image of the cross-section of a swath captured by the optical device (36, 38). 10. A tractor-baler combination (10) according to Claim 1 1 wherein the optical device is operatively coupled to a processor (41 ) that generates a predicted swath line derived from a plurality of two-dimensional images of the cross-section, silhouette or outline of a swath (24) captured sequentially by the optical device as the tractor moves. 1 1 . A tractor-baler combination (10) according to any preceding claim wherein the baler (13) is connected to the tractor by a drawbar (32) that is pivotably connected (a) at a tractor connection to the tractor (1 1 ) and (b) at a location spaced along the drawbar to an attachment location on the baler (12). 12. A method of controlling a moveable tractor-baler combination (10) including (a) a self-powered tractor (1 1 ) having a plurality of ground-engaging members (13, 14, 16, 17) and a steering mechanism for steering at least one said ground-engaging member so as to cause changes in a direction of movement of the tractor (1 1 ); (b) a baler (12) that is towed behind the tractor (1 1 ) as the tractor (1 1 ) moves forwardly and is pivotably connected to the tractor (1 1 ); (c) one or more sensors (36, 38) for sensing one or more lines (herein swath lines) of crop material corresponding to a maximal quantity of crop material per unit length of a swath (24); and (d) a control apparatus (41 ) that operates in dependence on at least one output of the one or more sensors (36, 38) to operate the steering mechanism of the tractor (1 1 ) such that the baler (12) follows a said swath line in a manner optimising the ingestion of crop material into the baler (12) for baling, wherein at least one said sensor (36, 38) is at least temporarily operable to sense a swath line that 

 is laterally offset from a direction of forward movement (44) of the tractor (1 1 ) (herein a laterally offset swath line), the method including causing the said sensor (36, 38) to sense a laterally offset swath line while the baler (12) is not following a line of a swath (24); and using the output of the said sensor (36, 38) as an input to the control apparatus (41 ) whereby to operate the steering mechanism so that the alignment of the baler (12) becomes optimised with respect to the laterally offset swath line before the ingestion of crop material commences. 13. A method according to Claim 12 in which one or more said sensor (36, 38) is moveable between a first orientation in which it points forwardly of the tractor (1 1 ) and a second orientation in which it points sideways of the tractor (1 1 ), the method including causing movement of the said sensor (36, 38) between the first and second orientations. 14. A method according to Claim 12 wherein the tractor-baler combination (10) includes respective first (36) and second (38) sensors for sensing one or more swath lines, the first sensor (36) pointing forwardly of the tractor (1 1 ) and the second sensor (38) pointing sideways of the tractor (1 1 ) and the orientations of the first and second sensors (36, 38) being fixed with respect to the tractor (1 1 ); and the method including causing the control apparatus (41 ) to operate the steering mechanism in dependence on the output of the second sensor (38) when the first sensor (36) does not sense a swath line (24). 15. A computer-readable medium containing instructions for carrying out a method according to Claim 12 or Claim 13.
